CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Continued To Mar-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ANDREW CHIU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant Andrew Chiu's Motion To Vacate Default is GRANTED.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), which allows a court to set aside a default based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. The record demonstrates excusable neglect and surprise. There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits. (Slusher v. Durrer (1977) 69 Cal.App.3d 747, 753.)

An attorney has an ethical obligation to warn an opposing party that the attorney is about to take their default. (Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 702.) "The ethical obligation to warn opposing counsel of an intent to take a default is now reinforced by a statutory policy that all parties 'cooperate in bringing the action to trial or other disposition.' ( 583.130.) Quiet speed and unreasonable deadlines do not qualify as 'cooperation' and cannot be accepted by the courts." (Lasalle, 36 Cal.App.5th at p. 137.) The court expects Plaintiffs and counsel to keep these rules and expectations in mind when faced with similar circumstances in the future.

Notwithstanding Moving Defendant's failure to lodge its proposed responsive pleading (see Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b)), the court finds good cause and orders the 10/15/2025 default against him set aside and grants Moving Defendant 10 court days leave to respond to the complaint, the time starting upon entry of this order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant Kent Mak's Motion To Vacate Default is GRANTED.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), which allows a court to set aside a default based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. The record demonstrates excusable neglect and surprise. There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits. (Slusher v. Durrer (1977) 69 Cal.App.3d 747, 753.)

Notwithstanding Moving Defendant's failure to lodge its proposed responsive pleading (see Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b)), the court finds good cause and orders the 10/15/2025 default against him set aside and grants Moving Defendant 10 court days leave to respond to the complaint, the time starting upon entry of this order.

Moving Defendant must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
